summary_one_line: Input must contain a well-formed HTTP response with a recognized content-encoding header so a writer stack is built and the cleanup loop iterates.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes forming the literal HTTP status-line/header tokens (e.g. 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 and a 'TranSfer'-Encoding header from 0x2c on) so curl parses a response and Curl_build_unencoding_stack pushes a contenc_writer; only then is writer non-NULL and the while(writer) true side taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact ASCII run 48(H)48(T)54(T)54(T)50(P)2f(/) at offsets 0x24-0x29 and a 'TranSfer'-style encoding token at 0x2c-0x3c; W coverage shows Curl_build_unencoding_stack and new_unencoding_writer executed (W hits 20/70, L hits 0), so the writer stack is non-empty and the loop body at 1017-1021 runs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35-73 bytes) '%'-percent-noise inputs lacking the HTTP/ and Transfer-Encoding literals at those offsets, so no writer is ever pushed (Curl_build_unencoding_stack L hits=0) and writer is NULL, taking the false side every time.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads intercepted string-CMP operands (curl's header-name comparisons against 'HTTP/', 'Transfer-Encoding') from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes those literals directly into the input, planting the magic tokens that build the writer stack; naive/value_profile have no I2SRandReplace and cannot synthesize the multi-byte literals by chance.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the while(writer) loop guard in Curl_unencode_cleanup whose true side is globally blocked.
Taking the true side requires the writer stack to be built, exercised only in winner runs.
Winner seeds contain the literal HTTP/ token at offsets 0x24-0x29.
Winner seeds carry a TranSfer-style header literal in the tail bytes.
Loser-blocking seeds are short percent-noise inputs without the HTTP literals.
The loser side never builds a writer, hitting the loop guard false-only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1016  T=70 F=20  T=0 F=20  while(writer) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a matched CMP operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the vpc>vp pair isolates the same I2S delta.
Both decisive pairs are labelled with the I2S delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 37 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0267a6030bf6fd21` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 49 of the 50-level ancestor chain, the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose while-loop guard depends on a multi-byte HTTP magic literal where naive/value_profile reach the true side as often and as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage).

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows the HTTP/ and Transfer literals are concentrated in winners, but I infer the writer stack is built from those exact tokens rather than verifying the precise header curl matched.
